title: trifolium stellatum subsp. stramineum
intro: Trifolium stellatum subsp. stramineum, also known as the golden clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Golden clover is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it can also be used in herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and salves.
